events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes an arc-flash rating below the task category - meaning the PPE/clothing selected has an arc-flash rating that is insufficient for the task category being performed. This is a PPE selection defect.

Key details:
- PPE selection under Safety & Rigging
- Arc-flash rating below task category (label vs. clothing)
- Rough-complete stage
- References: NFPA 70E, OSHA 1910 Subpart I

This is a non-compliant/defective condition - the selected PPE doesn't match the arc-flash hazard category for the task. This is dangerous and must fail.

NFPA 70E requires arc-rated clothing matching the hazard/risk category. OSHA 1910 Subpart I requires appropriate PPE selection based on hazard assessment.

Decision: fail
Risk: high or critical - arc-flash PPE below required rating is life-safety
Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant
Percent complete: rough-complete, maybe 70%
Value: 0 (no numeric reading requested specifically, though arc-flash category could be relevant - but we don't have specific numbers)
Confidence: high, 0.9
